q:
Rotational inertia of skater
A skater on ice begins to spin in the camel position, as shown in the figure to the left below. As she continues to spin, she straightens up, pulls her arms to her chest, and crosses her legs to spin in the corkscrew position, as shown in the figure to the right below. Air resistance and friction are negligible as the skater spins. Which of the following statements about the skater’s motion is true?
o: Her rotational inertia increases when she moves to the corkscrew position because her angular speed increases.
Her rotational inertia increases when she moves to the corkscrew position because her mass is distributed closer to her axis of rotation.
Her rotational inertia decreases when she moves to the corkscrew position because her angular speed decreases.
Her rotational inertia decreases when she moves to the corkscrew position because her mass is distributed closer to her axis of rotation.
Her rotational inertia remains the same when she moves to the corkscrew position because there is negligible frictional torque.

q:
Solid and hollow yo-yo
Two disks have equal masses and radii. Disk A is solid, and Disk B is hollow. Each disk has a thin string wrapped around it. Both strings are attached to the ceiling as shown. The disks are simultaneously released from rest from the same height. Which of the following accurately describes which disk will hit the ground first and why?
o: Disk A will hit the ground first. Disk A has a greater rotational inertia; therefore, the tension in the string for Disk A will be greater than the tension in the string for Disk B .
Disk B will hit the ground first. Disk B has a smaller rotational inertia; therefore, it will have a greater angular acceleration.
Disk A will hit the ground first. Disk A has a smaller rotational inertia; therefore, it will have a greater linear acceleration.
Disk B will hit the ground first. Disk B has a greater rotational inertia; therefore, it will have a greater angular acceleration.
Both disks will hit the ground at the same time. Since both disks have the same mass, they will have the same linear acceleration.
